name: router_sharpen_exp014
hypothesis: Phase-2 mechanism variant: the lane router (softmax over M=4 lanes) is zero-init -> starts uniform (each lane weight 0.25), so the readout = mean of all 4 lanes' states, diluting any single lane's binding signal 4x at init; if router gradients are weak (format shortcut satisfied by frozen full-attention layers), routing stays near-uniform and retrieval is diluted. A learnable router temperature (init 1.0, parity-equiv) lets routing sharpen over training to select the binding-holding lane. If tokacc climbs past 0.30, routing dilution was the bottleneck; if not, the bottleneck is structural (not a GDN3 mechanism) -> human handoff.
